notes: Could not run directly from Steam (menu/game crashes, depending on Proton version), but after running it with PROTON_DUMP_DEBUG_COMMANDS=1, I could run both games fine by going to its steam folder ("~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Zero Escape The Nonary Games") and running it with /tmp/proton_$USER/run "ze1.exe" (for 999 or "ze2.exe" for the VLR). Steam needs to be open in the background or the game will not load.
title: Zero Escape: The Nonary Games

notes: VLR works perfectly fine. The launcher doesn't work though, so I had to rename ze2.exe to Launcher.exe to bypass it. 999 isn't tested.
title: Zero Escape: The Nonary Games

notes: with proton 4.2-3 mono is build in and .net is no longer needed for the launcher to work but the launcher crashes when selecting fullscreen so i suggest to just use borderless instead. I've not tested the second game long enough but it seems to work just fine. 999 works well until the first door gets opened which is when the game will freeze is quartz is not installed. You can easily install quartz using protontricks just type "protontricks 477740 quartz" without the "" and you are good to go
title: Zero Escape: The Nonary Games

notes: Previously the launcher was wonky and the fullscreen option crashed the launcher itself. Now with 4.2-9 the launcher worked for me without any issues and games launched fine from the launcher. Dualshock 4 option for controller worked as well.
title: Zero Escape: The Nonary Games

notes: The Game starts, but is unable to play. After the logos came up there is only a black screen.
title: Zero Escape: The Nonary Games

notes:
The launcher works.  
VLR works perfectly , completely payed trough . 
999 starts but displays a blackscreen after pressing start.
title: Zero Escape: The Nonary Games

notes: Game would start up all right but get stuck in a black screen after starting a campaign. Worked perfectly after installing quartz with protontricks.
title: Zero Escape: The Nonary Games
